content: The metal-pair that can produce nascent hydrogen in alkaline medium is :
explanation:
Only Zn and Al produce nascent hydrogen with alkaline medium.

$$\mathrm{Zn+2NaOH}\,\longrightarrow\,\mathrm{Na_2ZnO_2+2[H]}$$

$$\mathrm{Al+2NaOH}\,\longrightarrow\,\mathrm{Na_2AlO_2+2[H]}$$
Tritium is the heaviest and only radioactive isotope of hydrogen.

content: How and why does the density of liquid water change on prolonged electrolysis?

options.content: Decreases, as the proportion of H<sub>2</sub>O increases
Increases, as the proportion of D<sub>2</sub>O increases

explanation: On prolonged electrolysis, the density of liquid water increases as the proportion of D<sub>2</sub>O increases. This is because on prolonged electrolysis, the concentration of heavy water (D<sub>2</sub>O) in liquid water increases which has higher density than ordinary water (H<sub>2</sub>O). If electrolysis continued, then almost pure D<sub>2</sub>O is obtained.

content: Which statement is not correct for ortho and para hydrogen?

options.content: Ortho-form is more stable than para-form
They differ in their nucleus spin
The ratio of ortho to para hydrogen changes with change in temperature

explanation:
(i) Ortho and para hydrogens have different nuclear spin.

In H2 molecule, two protons in two H atoms with parallel spin are called ortho-hydrogen



Nucleus of hydrogen atom with same spin

(ortho)

and with opposite spin are called para-hydrogen.



Nucleus of hydrogen atom with different spin

(para)

(ii) Ortho-hydrogen is more stable than para form at and above room temperature only, therefore para form always tends to revert in ortho-form. Thus, not correct.

(iii) The per cent composition of ortho and para changes with the temperature. Thus, their ratio also change.

(iv) They have slightly different boiling point.

Hence, (b) is the answer.
